_comment: Per-label recall thresholds for the ScanMeNow benchmark runner. Owned by Task #4 (Group C). PHI labels derived from hipaa_coverage.json confidence notes (Task #3). PII labels set by Task C based on Presidio built-in recognizer reliability profiles. Add new labels here when Task G (PII) or Task #16 (international PII) ships — runner picks them up without code changes.

_schema.min_recall: Minimum recall fraction (0.0–1.0) required for a passing benchmark run. null = no threshold (best-effort label).
_schema.hard_threshold: If true, falling below min_recall fails CI. If false, result is reported but does not block.
_schema.notes: Human-readable rationale for the threshold value.

thresholds.notes: High-precision RFC 5322 pattern. Native Presidio recognizer. 90% floor reflects format diversity in corpus (display names, quoted local parts).
Well-defined NNN-NN-NNNN format. Native Presidio recognizer. High confidence.
IPv4 dotted-decimal and IPv6 formats are unambiguous. Native Presidio recognizer.
http/https URLs and bare domain references. Native Presidio recognizer.
Covers phone and fax (same label). Native PhoneRecognizer. Lower than SSN/email due to international format diversity in corpus.
Multi-format dates (ISO, US, written month). Native DateRecognizer. Year-only dates are not HIPAA-covered and should not appear in golden_findings.
spaCy en_core_web_lg NER. May miss initials-only, single-token names without context, or non-Western name formats.
VIN (17-char NHTSA format) is high-precision. License plate patterns vary by state — lower recall expected for non-standard plate formats. Custom recognizer (Task #3).
spaCy NER. May miss bare zip codes, PO boxes, or highly abbreviated addresses without surrounding context.
Context-anchored custom recognizer (Task #3). Recall-biased tuning. 65% floor acknowledges high variability in MRN formats across healthcare systems.
Context-anchored custom recognizer (Task #3). Alphanumeric ID patterns anchored by beneficiary/member keywords.
Custom recognizer (Task #3). Broad recall-biased patterns; high false-positive tolerance. Supplements native US_BANK_NUMBER.
Custom recognizer (Task #3). Generic cert/license patterns; high format variability. Supplements native MEDICAL_LICENSE and US_DRIVER_LICENSE.
Custom recognizer (Task #3). Serial number formats vary enormously across device manufacturers. Context-keyword anchoring reduces but does not eliminate noise.
Best-effort keyword/phrase detection (Task #3). Detects text descriptions of biometric data, not actual biometric values. High false-negative rate expected. No threshold — results reported only.
Luhn-validated card numbers. Native Presidio recognizer. High-precision format; strong recall expected across Visa, Mastercard, Amex formats.
US routing + account number patterns. Native Presidio recognizer. Lower than credit card due to format variability (routing alone, account alone, combined).
9-digit alphanumeric US passport number. Native Presidio recognizer. Relatively distinctive format.
Native Presidio recognizer. State-issued license formats vary significantly (4–20 chars, alpha/numeric/mixed). Corpus must include multiple state formats; recall will vary by state.
SWIFT/IBAN format is well-standardised (15–34 alphanumeric chars with 2-char country code prefix). Native Presidio recognizer.
Cryptocurrency wallet addresses: Bitcoin (Base58, 26–35 chars), Ethereum (0x + 40 hex chars). Native Presidio recognizer. Recall varies by chain — Bitcoin and Ethereum expected highest.

NPI numbers (10-digit) and state medical license formats. Native Presidio recognizer. Overlaps intentionally with CERTIFICATE_LICENSE_NUMBER for NPI values — both may fire; both are acceptable. NPI is the primary target.

Nationality/religion/political group — keyword/phrase detection. Best-effort only; analogous to BIOMETRIC_IDENTIFIER. High false-negative rate for formal or indirect references. No threshold — results reported only.
